evals.prompt: 우리 팀이 다음 분기에 TypeScript로 전환하기로 결정했어. 반론 3가지를 들어줘.
evals.expected_output: 3개의 구체적이고 강력한 반론 제시. 각 반론은 TypeScript 전환의 실제 비용, 리스크, 대안을 구체적으로 다뤄야 함. 핵심 취약점 포함. Strawman이 아닌 steel-man 형태.
expectations: Exactly 3 counterarguments are presented (not more, not fewer unless justified)
Each counterargument is specific to TypeScript migration, not generic change-resistance arguments
A 'core vulnerability' or '핵심 취약점' section is present
The arguments are stated directly without excessive hedging ('some might argue...')
The response does NOT include a balanced 'but here are the benefits' section that undermines the adversarial framing

evals.prompt: Devil's advocate: we should sunset our mobile app and go web-only to reduce maintenance overhead.
evals.expected_output: 3 strong counterarguments against going web-only, covering user behavior, competitive positioning, and technical/business risks. Core vulnerability identified. No hedging, direct and specific.
expectations: Arguments are steel-manned — they represent the strongest possible case against web-only
A core vulnerability section is present identifying the deepest structural flaw
Arguments are specific to mobile-vs-web tradeoffs, not generic product strategy platitudes
Response is in English (matching the prompt language)

evals.prompt: 이 설계의 반대 입장 검토해줘: 우리 스타트업은 초기부터 멀티테넌트 SaaS 아키텍처로 시작해야 한다. 처음부터 제대로 만들어야 나중에 고생 안 한다.
evals.expected_output: 멀티테넌트 초기 도입에 대한 강력한 반론들. 초기 스타트업 맥락에서의 복잡성 비용, YAGNI 원칙, 피벗 가능성 등을 다뤄야 함. 핵심 취약점 포함.
expectations: Counterarguments are presented in Korean (matching prompt language)
Arguments address the specific startup context — not just 'multi-tenancy is complex' in the abstract
At least one argument challenges the 'premature optimization' nature of the claim
No section that softens the critique by restating the benefits of multi-tenancy
